title: Capability Discovery
concept: How a client asks a server what tools, resources, and prompts it offers before using any of them.

exercises.prompt: In our last lesson, we learned about MCP Prompts. What are they?

options.text: They are hardcoded system prompts that can never be changed.
They are reusable, parameterized templates that servers expose to clients.
They are security tokens used to log in to external web services.

exercises.explain: MCP Prompts are reusable templates provided by the server, allowing servers to expose custom prompt patterns to the client.

cards.title: What's in Store?
cards.body: Imagine walking into a store where all the labels are blank. You wouldn't know what you can buy! Software clients face the same issue until they ask the server for its capabilities.

cards.title: Capability Discovery
cards.body: Capability Discovery is the process where an MCP client asks a server for a list of its supported tools, resources, and prompts before attempting to use them. This ensures the client only sends requests the server actually knows how to handle.

cards.title: The Restaurant Menu
cards.body: Think of a restaurant menu. Before ordering, you read the menu to see what dishes (tools), ingredients (resources), and daily specials (prompts) are available today.

cards.title: Discovery in Action
cards.body: When an LLM coding assistant connects to a local database server, it first calls 'tools/list' and receives a list containing a 'run-query' tool, allowing the assistant to know it can query the database.

cards.title: No Hardcoding Required
cards.body: You might think a client has to hardcode every tool for every server it connects to. In reality, discovery allows the client to adapt dynamically to any new server it meets on the fly.

cards.title: Blind Guessing vs. Reading the Menu
cards.body: Comparing how a client behaves with and without performing a capability discovery phase.

left.text: The client guesses tools blindly, risking errors and broken calls.

right.text: The client reads the server's menu first, calling tools with confidence.

exercises.prompt: What does an MCP server return when a client asks to discover its capabilities?

options.text: A list of the user's personal files.
A list of its supported tools, resources, and prompt templates.
A copy of the LLM's neural network weights.
The source code of the entire client application.

exercises.explain: The server returns a structured list of tools, resources, and prompt templates it supports so the client knows what is available.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Capability discovery means a client does not need to pre-program the list of tools for every server it might connect to.
exercises.explain: Capability discovery allows a client to learn what a server can do dynamically, so the client doesn't need to know anything about the server beforehand.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of initial server communication:
exercises.sentence: Upon connection, the client asks the server to list its capabilities so it knows what is supported.
exercises.explain: Listing capabilities allows the client to know what tools, resources, and prompts are available right after connecting.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario shows a system performing dynamic Capability Discovery?

options.text: A developer writes a custom Python script that hardcodes the exact API endpoints for one specific weather service.
An LLM client queries a newly connected file-system server using `tools/list` to dynamically find out it can write and read local files.
A database administrator manually copies and pastes SQL queries into a terminal window to check if a database is online.

exercises.explain: The second option represents an AI assistant dynamically discovering capabilities via standard MCP list commands. The others describe manual configuration or static code.

exercises.prompt: How does an MCP client establish what actions it can perform using a newly connected server?

options.text: It automatically executes all tools on the server to test them.
It requests list endpoints to discover the available tools, resources, and prompts.
It bypasses the server and searches a public internet registry.
It forces the server to download new tools from the client.

exercises.explain: During capability discovery, the client reads the list of tools, resources, and prompts the server supports. The server does not execute these tasks yet; it simply advertises what it is capable of doing so the client knows how to interact with it.
